summary: Implemented S7 PR-13 hard cutover plus queue-path parity and shared frontend speed-formatting utility: per-subscription settings PATCH/UI/media inheritance and queue-driven first-play fallback now consistently honor subscription defaults when episode listening state is absent.
how_to_test: make migrate-test && cd python && DATABASE_URL=postgresql+psycopg://postgres:postgres@localhost:54322/nexus_test uv run pytest -q tests/test_podcasts.py::TestPodcastApiSurface::test_patch_subscription_settings_updates_contract_and_episode_default_speed tests/test_podcasts.py::TestPodcastApiSurface::test_patch_subscription_settings_rejects_out_of_range_default_speed tests/test_migrations.py::TestPlaybackQueueMigration::test_head_contains_playback_queue_table_and_auto_queue_flag tests/test_playback_queue.py::TestPlaybackQueueApi::test_queue_rows_expose_subscription_default_playback_speed tests/test_playback_queue.py::TestPlaybackQueueApi::test_post_items_supports_next_last_and_ignores_duplicates && cd ../apps/web && npm test -- "src/__tests__/components/GlobalPlayerQueue.test.tsx" "src/app/api/podcasts/podcasts-routes.test.ts" "src/app/(authenticated)/media/[id]/TranscriptMediaPane.test.tsx" "src/app/(authenticated)/podcasts/podcasts-flows.test.tsx"
risks: First-play speed inheritance relies on subscription_default_playback_speed hydration in media/queue payload paths used by playback entrypoints.
Non-podcast queue items intentionally surface no subscription speed and will continue to start at 1.0x unless a per-episode listening-state speed exists.
